vocabulary.meaningEn: to travel regularly between home and work

cards.prompt: I ______ to the office by train every morning.

examples.en: A long commute can drain your energy.
examples.uz: Uzoq yo'l energiyangizni so'rib olishi mumkin.

cards.prompt: A helpful ______ makes a hard project easier.

examples.en: Ask a colleague when you're stuck.

vocabulary.meaningEn: a move to a higher or more important job

cards.prompt: After two strong years, she earned a ______.

examples.en: He asked his manager about a promotion.
examples.uz: U rahbaridan lavozim ko'tarilishi haqida so'radi.

vocabulary.meaningEn: to discuss something in order to reach an agreement

cards.prompt: Learn to ______ your salary before you accept the offer.

examples.en: They negotiated a better deal for both sides.
examples.uz: Ular ikkala tomon uchun yaxshiroq shartnoma kelishdilar.

vocabulary.meaningEn: the amount of work a person has to do

cards.prompt: Talk to your manager if your ______ is too heavy.

examples.en: A balanced workload keeps quality high.
examples.uz: Muvozanatli ish hajmi sifatni yuqori saqlaydi.

vocabulary.meaningEn: to accept a job or responsibility

cards.prompt: Don't ______ ______ more than you can finish well.

examples.en: She took on the project and led it well.
examples.uz: U loyihani zimmasiga oldi va yaxshi boshqardi.

vocabulary.meaningEn: to take action to solve a problem or handle a situation

cards.prompt: A good manager helps the team ______ ______ problems calmly.

examples.en: Deal with the hardest task first.
examples.uz: Eng qiyin vazifani birinchi hal qiling.

vocabulary.meaningEn: able to be trusted to do what is expected

cards.prompt: Being ______ matters more than being brilliant once.

vocabulary.meaningEn: money you regularly receive from work or investments

cards.prompt: Spend less than your ______ and save the rest.

examples.en: A second income gives you more freedom.
examples.uz: Ikkinchi daromad sizga ko'proq erkinlik beradi.

vocabulary.meaningEn: money that you spend on something

cards.prompt: Track every ______ for a month to see where money goes.

examples.en: Cut one small expense and save it instead.
examples.uz: Bitta kichik xarajatni qisqartiring va o'rniga jamg'aring.

vocabulary.meaningEn: to plan how to spend your money

cards.prompt: Learn to ______ so your money lasts the whole month.

examples.en: Budget for fun, not just bills.
examples.uz: Faqat to'lovlar uchun emas, ko'ngilxushlik uchun ham reja tuzing.

vocabulary.meaningEn: to keep money over time for a particular purpose

cards.prompt: We're trying to ______ ______ for a holiday.

examples.en: Save up a small emergency fund first.

vocabulary.meaningUz: qurbi yetmoq, sotib olishga imkoni bo'lmoq
vocabulary.meaningEn: to have enough money or time for something

cards.prompt: Ask not just "do I want it?" but "can I ______ it?"
qurbi yetmoq, sotib olishga imkoni bo'lmoq (fe'l)

examples.en: Wait until you can truly afford it.

vocabulary.meaningUz: talabchan, og'ir, ko'p kuch talab qiladigan
vocabulary.meaningEn: needing a lot of time, effort, or attention

cards.prompt: It's a ______ job, so good rest is essential.
talabchan, og'ir, ko'p kuch talab qiladigan (sifat)

examples.en: A demanding week needs a calm weekend.
examples.uz: Og'ir hafta tinch dam olish kunlarini talab qiladi.

vocabulary.meaningEn: a healthy split between time spent working and time for personal life

examples.en: Good work-life balance protects your long-term energy.
examples.uz: Yaxshi ish-hayot muvozanati uzoq muddatli energiyangizni asraydi.

grammar.explanationMd:
## Present Perfect vs Past Simple

### Past simple — finished time
Use for actions at a **specific, finished** past time.
> I **saw** him **yesterday**. / She **started** the job **in 2020**.
Signal words: *yesterday, last week, in 2020, ago, when*.

### Present perfect — connected to now
Use for past actions with a **link to the present**, or an **unfinished** time period.
> I **have finished** the report. (it's done now)
> She **has worked** here **for** five years. (still works here)
Signal words: *already, just, yet, ever, never, since, for, so far*.

### Common mistakes
- ❌ *I have seen him yesterday.*
- ✅ *I saw him yesterday.* (finished time → past simple)

exercises.prompt: I ______ (finish) my work, so I can relax now.
She has started her new job last Monday.
exercises.answer: She started her new job last Monday.
exercises.explanationMd: *last Monday* is a finished time → **past simple**.

exercises.prompt: Build: (I / never / have / to / been / Japan)
exercises.answer: I have never been to Japan.

grammar.explanationMd:
## Relative Clauses

They add information about a noun using *who, which, that, whose, where*.

### Defining (essential) — no commas
Identifies which one; you can't remove it.
> The colleague **who helped me** got a promotion.
> The report **that I wrote** is on your desk. (*that* / *which* for things)

### Non-defining (extra info) — commas, no *that*
Adds extra detail; can be removed.
> My manager, **who is very kind**, approved it.

### which / who / whose / where
- *who* → people, *which* → things, *whose* → possession, *where* → places.

### Common mistakes
- ❌ *My boss, that is from Italy, is great.*
- ✅ *My boss, who is from Italy, is great.* (non-defining → not *that*)

exercises.prompt: That's the manager ______ hired me. (who / which)
This is the office which I work.
exercises.answer: This is the office where I work.
exercises.explanationMd: Use **where** for places (not *which* without a preposition).

exercises.prompt: She's the person ______ car was stolen.
Build: (the book / I / which / bought / is / useful / very)
exercises.answer: The book which I bought is very useful.

grammar.topic: Used to / be used to / get used to
grammar.explanationMd:
## Used to / be used to / get used to

Three different structures — easy to mix up.

### used to + base verb — past habit (not now)
> I **used to commute** by car. (I don't anymore)

### be used to + noun / -ing — already familiar
> I **am used to working** late. (it's normal for me now)

### get used to + noun / -ing — becoming familiar
> I'm slowly **getting used to the new schedule**.

### Common mistakes
- ❌ *I am used to work late.*
- ✅ *I am used to working late.* (be/get used to → **-ing** or noun)

exercises.prompt: I ______ (use to) live in a small town as a child.
She is used to work under pressure.
exercises.answer: She is used to working under pressure.
exercises.explanationMd: *be used to* takes **-ing** (working), not the base verb.

exercises.prompt: It was hard at first, but I'm ______ the early starts now.
Build: (he / used / to / smoke / but / quit / he)
exercises.answer: He used to smoke, but he quit.

speaking.promptEn: Describe your ideal job and the work-life balance you would want.
speaking.promptUz: Ideal ishingizni va xohlagan ish-hayot muvozanatingizni tasvirlang.
speaking.rubric: Encourage present/conditional structures and work vocabulary.

speaking.promptEn: Talk about how your money habits have changed. Use present perfect and past simple.
speaking.promptUz: Pul bilan bog'liq odatlaringiz qanday o'zgargani haqida gapiring. Present perfect va past simple ishlating.
speaking.rubric: Contrast present perfect (changes up to now) with past simple (finished moments).

speaking.promptEn: What did you use to do at work or school that you don't do now? What are you getting used to?
speaking.promptUz: Ilgari ishda yoki maktabda nima qilardingiz, hozir esa qilmaysiz? Nimaga o'rganib boryapsiz?
speaking.rubric: Encourage used to (past habit) and getting used to (becoming familiar).
